violations.text: Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. PIC had sanitizer diluted to between 200-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

Rodenticide was observed being used in basement storage area. Rodenticide is to be kept within a tamper resistant bait station. PIC removed rodenticide at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

Inspector observed a dog kept in a cage in a food preparation area. Live animals may not be allowed on the PREMISES of a FOOD ESTABLISHMENT. However, employee's service animal may be allowed in areas that are not used for food preparation and that are usually open for customers, such as dining and sales areas. PIC utilized partition to separate food prep area with front customer service area and ensured that cage is only accessible from the service area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The plumbing under the three-compartment sink was observed in poor repair (active leak observed). PIC instructed to have plumbing repaired by correct by date. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2026
The menu is lacking an allergy statement to customers notifying them to inform staff of any food allergies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sinks in the prep and ware-washing aras. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Staff failed to sanitize equipment after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Pureed vegetables and other foods in the walk-in cooler were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a dead mouse and an accumulation of small and large rodent droppings observed in the basement storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The screen door to the outside, located in the back entrance, was observed with a hole toward the bottom of the screen. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the three-compartment sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the prep tables, storage containers, shelving, and other equipment have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Employees' personal belongings were observed stored in the prep and food storage areas. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
